programme.title: Saturdays In the South: A History of SEC Football
programme.desc: The 1990s bring change, with expansion and the sport's first-ever conference championship game. Florida's Steve Spurrier fashions an era of high-octane offense, and his Gators form a rivalry with Tennessee and QB Peyton Manning.

programme.title: Saturdays In the South: A History of SEC Football
programme.desc: Nick Saban comes to LSU in 2000 and eventually wins the Tigers' first national championship since 1958 - a feat matched by Les Miles. At Florida, Tim Tebow becomes a cult hero and wins two national titles with Urban Meyer's Gators.

programme.title: Saturdays In the South: A History of SEC Football
programme.desc: The SEC has a run of seven straight national titles, and nine in 12 years. Expansion in 2012 gives the conference 14 schools. Meanwhile, Nick Saban arguably becomes the greatest coach in the history of college football.

programme.title: TrueSouth
programme.desc: The subject of endless magazine listicles and breathless influencer reels; is it possible to sidestep the algorithms and really see and hear and taste Charleston?

programme.title: Live: Unsportsmanlike with Evan, Canty and Michelle
programme.desc: Start the day off right with Chris Canty, Evan Cohen and Michelle Smallmon providing an energizing and informative way to take on the day. Sports, life and laughter are what it's all about.

programme.title: 30 for 30
programme.desc: The fascinating backstory behind the famous/infamous 1988 "Catholics vs. Convicts" college football game between Notre Dame and the University of Miami. The slogan summarized the subtext of cultural and racial tensions brewing between the schools.

programme.title: 30 for 30
programme.desc: Southern Methodist University's football program was riding high in the early '80s -- only to receive the "death penalty" from the NCAA after numerous rules violations.

programme.title: 30 for 30
programme.desc: Filmmaker Billy Corben documents the rise to prominence of the college football program at the University of Miami.

programme.title: 30 for 30
programme.desc: The University of Miami's football program tries to recover from the devastation left by NCAA sanctions and scandals.

programme.title: 30 for 30
programme.desc: The polarizing beliefs and on-field successes of former Colorado football coach Bill McCartney.

programme.title: 30 for 30
programme.desc: An exploration of the class and power dynamics in college sports through the interconnected journeys of one-time dynamic running back Maurice Clarett and former elite head coach Jim Tressel.

programme.title: SEC Storied
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of ex-NFL star Steve Spurrier, one of the most fascinating and competitive men in college football.

programme.title: SEC Storied
programme.desc: Ole Miss defensive back Chucky Mullins and Vanderbilt running back Brad Gaines are brought together by tragedy.

programme.title: 30 for 30
programme.desc: The rise, fall and maturation of former Oklahoma University All-American linebacker Brian Bosworth.

programme.title: 30 for 30
programme.desc: The 1983 NFL Draft was highlighted by a record six quarterbacks being taken in the first round, including John Elway and Dan Marino.

programme.title: Live: Unsportsmanlike with Evan, Canty and Michelle
programme.desc: Start the day off right with Chris Canty, Evan Cohen and Michelle Smallmon providing an energizing and informative way to take on the day. Sports, life and laughter are what it's all about.

programme.title: College Football
programme.desc: The Tigers (5-3, 2-3 SEC) try to put a tumultuous period behind them as they visit the No. 4 Crimson Tide (7-1, 5-0). LSU dismissed head coach Brian Kelly and athletic director Scott Woodward in the days following a 49-25 loss to Texas A&M.

programme.title: College Football
programme.desc: The No. 11 Sooners (7-2, 3-2 SEC) visit the No. 4 Crimson Tide (8-1, 6-0) in this marquee matchup. Alabama has won eight straight after burying LSU, 20-9 out of its bye Nov. 8. Oklahoma shocked then-No. 14 Tennessee 33-27 before its week off Nov. 1.

programme.title: College Football
programme.desc: SEC rivals in the No. 10 Crimson Tide (9-2, 6-1) and Tigers (5-6, 1-6) meet in the Iron Bowl. Alabama leads the series 51-37-1. The Crimson Tide will earn a berth in the SEC championship game if they beat the Tigers for the sixth straight time.

programme.title: College Football
programme.desc: The No. 9 Crimson Tide (10-2) and No. 3 Bulldogs (11-1) meet in the SEC championship game for the third time in five years. Alabama is 4-0 against Georgia in SEC title contests. The Bulldogs yielded a total of 22 points in the last three outings.

programme.title: Live Test Cricket
programme.desc: Ireland v New Zealand. Coverage of the first day of the one-off Test from Civil Service Cricket Club in Stormont, Belfast. This is just the third Test match to be played in Ireland and just the 13th game that they have played in history.
